justification: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classification (BR-1 KYC, BR-2 unverified receive-only, BR-5/BR-6 daily limits by tier, BR-7 settlement irreversibility, BR-8 sanctions screening, BR-9/BR-10 login lockout, BR-11 notification marked invariant: false). Notification rule correctly marked as soft/false. Sanctions rule included as invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced in Low-Confidence Areas. 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level 78% falls within the 60-85 range with detailed assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 rules identified and expanded to 11 granular rules with correct invariant classification
Notification rule (BR-11) correctly marked invariant: false with clear rationale distinguishing it from state-corrupting violations
Sanctions screening (BR-8) correctly identified as hard invariant with additional insight about not tipping off the user (31 CFR § 501.602)
Timezone ambiguity for daily limit reset explicitly called out in Low-Confidence Areas
10 acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the 4 minimum
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified limits and flags the withdrawal ambiguity for partially-verified as an assumption
Excellent edge cases including race conditions on limit checks, sanctions list updates mid-pending, and login counter reset on success
Confidence level 78% is well-calibrated within the expected 60-85 range
Rich ubiquitous language glossary and bounded context identification add domain modeling value
AC-10 catches the subtle >$1,000 vs ≥$1,000 boundary condition

# Domain Analysis — Digital Wallet

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This encodes a **digital wallet / money transfer system** — a regulated fintech product that allows users to store value, send money to other users or entities, receive funds, and withdraw to external accounts. The system operates under financial compliance obligations (KYC/AML) and must enforce transfer limits, sanctions screening, and account security controls.
